briefing.headline: Pitch it tight — winter clear water puts a jig in its best element.
briefing.gameplan:
In winter with clear water and post frontal conditions, jigs presentations shine.

Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, creek channel, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Drop the bait to about 2 ft above the fish's head — smallmouth were feeding up, so dropping it in front of them on the bottom meant they wouldn't bite. Keep bait above their heads and they co
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, creek channel, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Hackney: 'For winter deep-water jig fishing on lowland reservoirs with stumps and channel swings in 6-8 feet, I use 16-20 pound fluorocarbon. On hard structure like rock I go 14-16 pound. Only downsize to 12 in extremely deep clear water'

KVD: 'Winter bass move shallower than most realize — if you can reach them with crankbait, jerkbait, or slow-rolled spinnerbait in 10ft or less, power baits are first option. Fish avoid expansive flats unless warming trend pushes them to gravel po...

Wheeler: Big fish spots are big giant flats in 22-29 ft with tiny isolated rockpiles — a boulder in the middle of a rocky flat, or a hard-bottom spot. Only good for 1-2 fish and time-consuming to find, but that's what separates you

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal winter is the toughest bite of the year. Fish retreat to the deepest structure available (20-35ft+) and barely move. Target steep structure where fish can access deep water without traveling — bluffs, vertical channel walls, bridge pilings.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize lures and switch to brighter colors
If bites are subtle, try a lighter jig weight — slower fall rate can be the difference
